front: Movement: nicknamed 'Gherardo delle Notti' for use of small visible light sources; associated with The Denial of St. Peter and Christ Before the High Priest
Artist: painted a scene in which the view of a woman's candle is blocked by a guard's arm while she holds the robe of the title figure
Artist: painted The Deposition (San Pietro in Montorio, Rome), in which a pale hand points to a tool basket and crown of thorns as three distraught women watch the entombment
Artist: painted several musician subjects including the Singing Lute Player and Flute Player
Artist: painted The Procuress; member of a Dutch Baroque movement named for a city
Movement: noted for depictions of prostitutes including the Scene of Venal Love (smiling bare-chested woman grabbed by an old man) and The Procuress
Movement: influenced Aelbert Cuyp's golden lighting in seascape paintings like The Maas at Dordrecht
